LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BRINK'S INCORPORATED'S MOTION TO SEVER PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS IS GRANTED. THE CLAIMS OF PLAINTIFF ERNIE FERNANDEZ, WHICH SHALL HAVE ITS OWN CASE NUMBER, ARE SEVERED FROM THE CLAIMS OF THE SEVEN OTHER PLAINTIFFS. THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGE THAT THE REST AND MEAL PERIOD CLAIMS OF ALL PLAINTIFFS OTHER THAN MR. FERNANDEZ ARISE OUT OF THE SAME TRANSACTION AND SHARE A COMMON FACT TO PERMIT JOINDER OF THE CLAIMS OF ALL PLAINTIFFS OTHER THAN MR. FERNANDEZ. BRINK'S REQUEST FOR DISCRETIONARY SEVERANCE IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO RENEWING THE REQUEST TO THE TRIAL JUDGE. THE TRIAL JUDGE, NOT A LAW AND MOTION JUDGE, WILL BE IN THE BEST POSITION TO DETERMINE IF FURTHER SEVERANCE IS WARRANTED BASED ON THE RELEVANT FACTORS, INCLUDING JUDICIAL ECONOMY AND AVOIDANCE OF PREJUDICE TO ANY PARTY AND JUROR CONFUSION. THE TRIAL DATE REMAINS AND SHALL APPLY ALSO TO THE NEW CASE CREATED FOR PLAINTIFF ERNIE FERNANDEZ. PLAINTIFF IS INSTRUCTED TO FILE A NEW COMPLAINT ALONG WITH A CIVIL CASE COVERSHEET AND SUMMONS. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. UPON FILING OF THE ORDER, COUNSEL SHALL DELIVER A COPY OF THE ORDER TO THE FILING WINDOW CLERK, ROOM 103 OF THE SAN FRANCISCO CIVIC CENTER COURTHOUSE. THIS WILL ENABLE THE CLERK TO DISPOSE OF AN AUTOMATICALLY-SCHEDULED CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE TO ALSO SET THE TRIAL DATE CONCURRENT WITH THE EXISTING CASE 555893. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR NO. 6576, E-MAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-12-2019 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-11-2018 Continued To Jul-22-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-23-2018 Continued To Jan-28-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Aug-23-2017 Set For Trial On Aug-13-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Sever Plaintiffs' Claims
Off Calendar Nov-13-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Daniel P. Ianitelli of the law offices of Liberation Law Group, PC (415-695-1000) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Susan T. Ye of the law offices of Ogletree Deakins (415-442-4810) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

DEFENDANT BRINK'S INCORPORATED'S Motion To Sever Plaintiffs' Claims is GRANTED. The claims of plaintiff Ernie Fernandez, which shall have its own case number, are severed from the claims of the seven other plaintiffs. The second and third causes of action sufficiently allege that the rest and meal period claims of all plaintiffs other than Mr. Fernandez arise out of the same transaction and share a common fact to permit joinder of the claims of all plaintiffs other than Mr. Fernandez. Brink's request for discretionary severance is denied without prejudice to renewing the request to the trial judge. The trial judge, not a law and motion judge, will be in the best position to determine if further severance is warranted based on the relevant factors, including judicial economy and avoidance of prejudice to any party and juror confusion. The trial date remains and shall apply also to the new case created for plaintiff Ernie Fernandez. Plaintiff is instructed to file a new complaint along with a Civil Case Coversheet and summons. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Instructions to Counsel: Upon filing of the order, counsel shall deliver a copy of the order to the filing window clerk, Room 103 of the San Francisco Civic Center Courthouse. This will enable the clerk to dispose of an automatically-scheduled case management conference to also set the trial date concurrent with the existing case 555893.
